name: TikTok Competitor Monitor with Scavio + Google Sheets

parameters.content:
## TikTok Competitor Monitor with Scavio + Google Sheets

### How it works
This workflow monitors competitor TikTok accounts for new posts. Every 6 hours it reads usernames from a Google Sheet, fetches their latest videos via Scavio, compares against the last known video ID, and logs every new post with its description, metrics, and direct link to a Posts tab. If new content is found, you get a Gmail alert.

Powered by [Scavio](https://scavio.dev), a real-time search API for Google, Amazon, Walmart, YouTube, Reddit, and TikTok. Free tier: 250 credits per month.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Install the **Scavio** community node: Settings -> Community Nodes -> install `n8n-nodes-scavio`.
- [ ] Get a free Scavio API key at https://dashboard.scavio.dev (250 credits/mo).
- [ ] Create a Google Sheet with two tabs:
  - **Competitors**: columns `username`, `sec_user_id` (leave blank), `last_video_id` (leave blank). Add one competitor per row.
  - **Posts**: columns `username`, `description`, `views`, `likes`, `comments`, `shares`, `link`, `detected_at`.
- [ ] Attach credentials: Scavio API on **Get Profile** + **Get Latest Posts**, Google Sheets OAuth2 on all sheet nodes, Gmail OAuth2 on **Alert new posts**.
- [ ] Open sheet nodes and pick your spreadsheet/tabs.
- [ ] Open **Alert new posts** and set `sendTo` to your email.
- [ ] Activate the workflow.

### Tips
- The first run seeds `last_video_id` without alerting. Alerts start on the second run.
- Each competitor costs 2 credits per run. 5 competitors every 6h fits the free tier.
- Build charts in Google Sheets from the Posts tab to compare competitor posting frequency and engagement.

### How can we improve this workflow?
### [>>> Share your feedback at scavio.dev/feedback](https://scavio.dev)

## 1. Read competitors

Fires every 6 hours and reads every row from the "Competitors" tab (one row = one TikTok account to monitor).

## 2. Check for new posts

For each competitor, resolves the sec_user_id via Get Profile, fetches the 5 newest posts, and compares the latest video_id against the stored last_video_id to detect new content.

## 3. Alert, log & save

If new posts are found, sends a Gmail alert, logs each post with description, metrics, and link to the Posts tab. Always saves sec_user_id and last_video_id back to the Competitors tab.
